event: pocket infection of his cardiac resynchronization therapy defibrillator
discomfort around his left upper chest implant site
echocardiography revealed severely reduced left ventricular systolic function
global hypokinesis of the left ventricle
preoperative computed tomography revealed that his leads were scarred to the lateral wall of the superior vena cava
cardiac resynchronization therapy defibrillator pocket capsule was dissected out
coronary sinus and right atrial leads extracted
transesophageal echocardiography revealed a large pericardial effusion
5-mm tear in the superior cavoatrial junction
oozing hematoma at the level of the innominate vein
lesions repaired with multiple 4-0 polypropylene sutures
right ventricular lead capped and abandoned
transfusions of cryoprecipitate, platelets, fresh frozen plasma, and factor VII
postoperatively transferred to the intensive care unit
required large doses of vasopressin, epinephrine, and norepinephrine
upper- and lower-digit ischemia progressed to dry gangrene
no ability to move his fingers and toes
liver enzymes returned to normal limits
laser extraction of his retained lead
pus drained from the subfascial area
microbial cultures grew Enterobacter cloacae and Staphylococcus epidermidis
evaluation of his hands and feet
no signs of local infection or wet gangrene
black skin changes and demarcation lines
frank mummification of his digits and toes
amputation and debridement of his necrotic feet
